title: Add rate limiting to auth endpoints
description:
## Issue
No rate limiting on authentication endpoints allows brute-force attacks.

## Requirements
- Add rate limiting middleware for auth endpoints
- Requires new dependency and design work — track separately

## Files
- crates/wifisync-server/src/api/routes.rs
- crates/wifisync-server/Cargo.toml

title: Make bcrypt cost configurable
description:
## Issue
Bcrypt uses DEFAULT_COST (12), not configurable. Making it configurable allows tuning per deployment.

## Requirements
- Add BCRYPT_COST env var to ServerConfig
- Default to 12, validate range 4-31

## Files
- crates/wifisync-server/src/config.rs
- crates/wifisync-server/src/api/handlers/auth.rs:39,70

title: Fix Docker health check to verify HTTP server
description:
## Issue
Current HEALTHCHECK uses 'sqlite3 /data/wifisync.db SELECT 1' which only verifies the database file exists. A hung HTTP server process passes this check.

## Requirements
- Change health check to curl the /health endpoint
- Add curl to the runtime Docker image
- Verify the HTTP server is actually responding

## Files
- Dockerfile:97-98

title: Enforce JWT expiration during token validation
description:
## Issue
Validation::default() in jsonwebtoken crate behavior needs verification. The security review claims it does NOT validate token expiration by default, allowing expired tokens to be decoded before the is_expired() check.

## Requirements
- Verify Validation::default() behavior for exp claim
- If not validated, explicitly set validate_exp = true
- Add test proving expired tokens are rejected at the decode level
- Clean up redundant is_expired() check if decode already handles it

## Files
- crates/wifisync-server/src/api/auth.rs:63-70
- crates/wifisync-server/src/api/auth.rs:106-108

title: Enable SQLite WAL mode for better concurrency
description:
## Issue
SQLite not using WAL mode. WAL improves concurrent read performance for multi-client sync server.

## Requirements
- Enable WAL mode via PRAGMA after database connection
- Add test

## Files
- crates/wifisync-server/src/db/mod.rs:12-15

title: Make CORS configuration restrictive by default
description:
## Issue
CORS allows all origins, methods, and headers. While this server is primarily for mobile/CLI (where CORS is irrelevant), it's good practice to restrict.

## Requirements
- Make CORS origins configurable via CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS env var
- Restrict allowed methods to GET, POST, DELETE
- Restrict allowed headers to Content-Type and Authorization

## Files
- crates/wifisync-server/src/api/routes.rs:18-21
- crates/wifisync-server/src/config.rs

title: Reject empty JWT_SECRET environment variable
description:
## Issue
If JWT_SECRET="" (empty string) is explicitly set, env::var() succeeds and returns empty string. An empty secret makes JWTs trivially forgeable since anyone can sign tokens with an empty key.

## Requirements
- Treat empty JWT_SECRET same as unset (generate random or reject)
- Add validation that panics/errors on startup if JWT_SECRET is empty in production
- Add test

## Files
- crates/wifisync-server/src/config.rs:31-40
